description: This figure displays SEM micrographs and EPMA elemental mapping for Fe–30Mn–8Al–1C steel containing 1 wt% Cu (a) and 3 wt% Cu (b), illustrating the microstructural role of Cu addition. Panel (a) shows secondary electron images with corresponding Al, Nb, Cu, C, and Mo maps for 1Cu: Nb and C co-segregate at white particles attached to the austenite matrix, consistent with NbC-type carbides, while Cu exhibits slight grain-boundary enrichment. Panel (b) presents the same suite of maps for 3Cu, revealing pronounced Cu enrichment along austenite grain boundaries and uneven Al distribution at certain boundaries; here, grain-boundary white particles are identified as elemental Cu, whereas matrix-surface particles correspond to residual NbC carbides. The figure demonstrates that increasing Cu content transforms the precipitate population from NbC-dominated surface carbides to substantial Cu grain-boundary segregation, providing compositional evidence for the microgalvanic corrosion sites described in the study.
